items.q: What is a tone row in serial music?
items.correct: An ordered series of all 12 chromatic pitches, used as the basis of a piece.
items.misconception: A row of notes played together as a chord.
items.random_wrong: Any sequence of notes a composer chooses.
items.why: Students miss the SPECIFIC structure. Schoenberg's 12-tone technique uses ALL 12 pitches in a fixed order before any repeats, generating melodies and harmonies from that row.

items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: Which chords form a plagal cadence?
items.why: Students confuse plagal (IV-I) with perfect (V-I) because both end on I. The 'amen' at the end of hymns is the classic plagal cadence - subdominant to tonic, with a softer ecclesiastical feel.

items.q: Where does the djembe come from?
items.why: It's West Africa. “China” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: On the off-beat. “On the beat” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Roughly which decade did Bob Marley make reggae global?
items.why: The real figure is 1970. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
